Title: The Landscape of Autophagy-Related (ATG) Genes and Functional Characterization of TaVAMP727 to Autophagy in Wheat

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The functional assays demonstrate that TaVAMP727 acts as a positive regulator of abiotic stress tolerance and heading in both heterologous (Arabidopsis) and homologous (wheat) systems by modulating ATG gene expression; however, direct protein–protein interaction partners of TaVAMP727 were not identified in this study.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of TaVAMP727 enhances tolerance to cold, drought and salt stresses and promotes earlier heading in wheat, and improves germination and stress tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis by altering the expression of multiple ATG genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Overexpression of TaVAMP727 is proposed to promote SNARE-mediated fusion of autophagosomes with the tonoplast in the late stage of autophagy, accelerating intracellular material and energy recycling; the resulting enhancement of autophagic flux alters the expression levels of multiple ATGs to maintain autophagic homeostasis, especially under cold, drought and salt stresses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in the autophagy pathway downstream of ATG complexes, likely facilitating SNARE-mediated autophagosome–vacuole fusion and thereby contributing to abiotic stress responses and developmental regulation such as heading.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Autophagy pathway, particularly SNARE-mediated fusion of autophagosomes with the tonoplast/vacuole during vesicular trafficking.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: TaVAMP727 --(activation)--> AtATG1A and AtATG6 under salt stress in Arabidopsis; TaVAMP727 --(mixed activation and repression)--> multiple Arabidopsis ATGs (5 up-regulated and 9 down-regulated under salt; 5 up-regulated and 5 down-regulated under drought); TaVAMP727 --(regulation)--> most TaATGs in wheat under cold, drought and salt stresses, with general up-regulation under cold and drought and a distinct expression pattern under salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vesicle-associated membrane protein 727 (VAMP727), a seed plant-specific R-SNARE involved in vacuolar transport, plant growth and seed maturation.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In non-transgenic wheat (Fielder), TaVAMP727 expression remained stable under normal, cold, drought and salt stress conditions. In TaVAMP727-overexpressing wheat, TaVAMP727 transcripts were strongly elevated under normal conditions and significantly down-regulated under cold, drought and salt stresses, but still remained much higher than in Fielder. In TaVAMP727-overexpressing Arabidopsis, TaVAMP727 expression was significantly up-regulated under salt stress and down-regulated under drought (D-mannitol) stress compared with control conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress (4 °C for 12 h), drought stress (air-dry for 6 h in wheat; 200–415 mM D-mannitol in Arabidopsis media; soil water withholding until wilting followed by rewatering), and salt stress (150 mM NaCl for 24 h in wheat; 120–180 mM NaCl in Arabidopsis germination and expression assays).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Autophagy-related regulation of abiotic stress tolerance and heading date in wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, salt); flowering time/heading date; autophagy-related plant development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In wheat, TaVAMP727-overexpressing lines headed at least six days earlier than Fielder and had a higher survival rate than Fielder under drought stress; in Arabidopsis, overexpression significantly increased germination rate under 415 mM D-mannitol and 180 mM NaCl (ANOVA with significant differences at p < 0.05 or p < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Transgenic wheat overexpressing TaVAMP727 shows higher seedling survival under drought stress; in Arabidopsis, overexpression increases germination rate under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia (Col-0) and TaVAMP727-overexpressing T3 lines; wheat cultivar Fielder and TaVAMP727-overexpressing T3 transgenic wheat lines.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Fielder (background cultivar for transgenic wheat lines)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of TaVAMP727 coding sequence from wheat cDNA; construction of overexpression vectors under ED35S or CaMV 35S-derived promoters; Agrobacterium tumefaciens-mediated transformation of Arabidopsis (floral dip) and wheat (immature embryo transformation); stress treatments including NaCl and D-mannitol in MS medium for Arabidopsis and cold (4 °C, 12 h), air-drying (6 h), and 150 mM NaCl (24 h) for wheat; germination rate assays; seedling drought-withholding and rewatering survival assays; heading date recording; RT-qPCR with UBQ5 (Arabidopsis) or GA3PD (wheat) as reference genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana (Col-0) and wheat (cv. Fielder) using Agrobacterium-mediated transformation, followed by stress-germination assays, seedling drought survival assays, heading date measurement, and RT-qPCR analysis of TaVAMP727 and ATG gene expression under control and abiotic stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TaVAMP727 is a promising transgenic and possibly marker-assisted breeding target to develop wheat cultivars with enhanced tolerance to cold, drought and salt stresses and earlier heading, enabling stable yield and potential expansion of wheat cultivation into colder, drier and saltier environments.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Hap-CGA likely represents a transitional haplotype preserved during evolution from wild emmer to durum, and the association between Ta2A_ATG8a promoter haplotypes and seed size is strong in tetraploid wheat but not fully applicable to hexaploid wheat, where seed size appears to be controlled by multiple loci across A, B and D subgenomes.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing SNP data from 93 accessions of wheat and its diploid and tetraploid progenitors used to identify TaATG SNPs, including those in the Ta2A_ATG8a promoter; π and fst values calculated from resequencing data.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural variation in cis-acting element–changing SNPs in the promoter of Ta2A_ATG8a defines three haplotypes whose distribution in tetraploid wheat populations is associated with differences in seed size: Hap-TAA confers the highest thousand kernel weight and grain width but shortest grain length, Hap-CGA is intermediate, and Hap-CGC is associated with lower thousand kernel weight.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Promoter SNPs alter the presence of cis-elements such as GAGAC (S-deficiency-responsive), TTATTT (linked to glutamine synthetase gene regulation important for seed germination and yield structure), and CAAT and other motifs, suggesting that selection on these cis-elements modulates Ta2A_ATG8a regulation and thereby contributes to differences in grain size between wild emmer and durum.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Autophagy-related processes influencing nutrient remobilization and stress responses, with promoter variation linked to seed development and grain size during tetraploid wheat domestication.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Autophagy pathway, with subfamily-level evidence for involvement in nitrogen starvation and abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Three cis-acting element–changing SNPs in the promoter of Ta2A_ATG8a on chr2A (e.g., positions 221198307 C/T, 221198687 G/A, 221199047 A/C) that define Hap-CGC, Hap-CGA and Hap-TAA, modify motifs such as GAGAC, GRWAAW, GATAA, GATA, AGAAA, TTATTT and CAAT, and are associated with variation in thousand kernel weight and grain morphology in tetraploid wheat; genomic position: promoter; reported impact: affects_function (seed size).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Association of promoter variation of an autophagy-related gene with seed size and domestication-related selection in tetraploid wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: grain yield component traits (thousand kernel weight, grain width, grain length); domestication-related seed size variation
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Hap-TAA promoter haplotype of Ta2A_ATG8a, associated with the highest thousand kernel weight and grain width in tetraploid wheat, representing a favorable allele for increased seed size; Hap-CGA confers intermediate but significantly higher TKW than Hap-CGC and likely represents a transitional haplotype preserved during domestication.
Plant_Genes.Superior_Haplotype: Hap-TAA at the Ta2A_ATG8a promoter is a superior haplotype conferring larger seed size (higher TKW and grain width) in tetraploid wheat compared with Hap-CGC and Hap-CGA.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Tetraploid wheat accessions carrying Hap-TAA showed the highest thousand kernel weight (TKW) and grain width but the shortest grain length; accessions with Hap-CGA had TKW significantly higher than those with Hap-CGC (p < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Seed size association with Ta2A_ATG8a promoter haplotypes is evident in tetraploid wheat but not fully applicable to hexaploid wheat, where seed size appears to be determined by multiple genes from the A, B and D subgenomes.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tetraploid wheat accessions including wild emmer and durum from laboratory collections; grain traits (TKW, grain length and width) measured with a SC-G seed test instrument.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Extraction of SNPs in promoter regions of TaATGs from whole-genome resequencing data; identification of cis-acting element–changing SNPs in the Ta2A_ATG8a promoter; definition of three haplotypes (Hap-CGC, Hap-CGA, Hap-TAA); measurement of thousand kernel weight, grain length and grain width; statistical comparison of traits among haplotypes using ANOVA and significance testing.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Haplotype-based association analysis of promoter SNPs in Ta2A_ATG8a using resequencing-derived SNP data, definition of three promoter haplotypes, cis-acting element prediction, and statistical association of haplotypes with grain traits (TKW, grain width, grain length) in wild emmer and durum tetraploid wheat populations.
Plant_Genes.Genetic_Population: Tetraploid wheat panel composed of wild emmer and durum accessions used to evaluate the association between Ta2A_ATG8a promoter haplotypes and grain traits.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Ta2A_ATG8a promoter haplotypes, particularly Hap-TAA, provide useful molecular markers for selecting tetraploid wheat lines with higher thousand kernel weight and favorable grain width, and the seed size–related cis-elements GAGAC and TTATTT in its promoter are prime candidates for marker-assisted selection and functional improvement during domestication and breeding.
